Web search results for query: "CSS masonry layout 2024 2025 grid-template-rows masonry native browser support alternatives"

Links: [{"title":"CSS Masonry Layout: Native Grid Support","url":"https://www.sitepoint.com/css-masonry-layout-native-grid/"},{"title":"Masonry layout - CSS - MDN Web Docs","url":"https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/Guides/Grid_layout/Masonry_layout"},{"title":"Making a Masonry Layout That Works Today | CSS-Tricks","url":"https://css-tricks.com/making-a-masonry-layout-that-works-today/"},{"title":"CSS Grid Lanes: The New Native Masonry Layout","url":"https://blog.openreplay.com/css-grid-lanes-masonry-layout/"},{"title":"Help us choose the final syntax for Masonry in CSS | WebKit","url":"https://webkit.org/blog/16026/css-masonry-syntax/"},{"title":"CSS Layout Patterns in 2026: Grid, Flexbox, or Bento?","url":"https://artofstyleframe.com/blog/css-layout-patterns-grid-flexbox-2026/"},{"title":"CSS Masonry Native 2025: Gapless Glory - BYBOWU","url":"https://bybowu.com/article/masonry-layouts-evolve-in-css-from-clunky-hacks-to-native-glory-redesign-your-galleries-overnight"},{"title":"39 Creative CSS Masonry Layouts","url":"https://wpdean.com/css-masonry/"},{"title":"Masonry In CSS: Should Grid Evolve Or Stand Aside For A New Module? — Smashing Magazine","url":"https://www.smashingmagazine.com/2025/05/masonry-css-should-grid-evolve-stand-aside-new-module/"},{"title":"Native CSS masonry layout | pawelgrzybek.com","url":"https://pawelgrzybek.com/native-css-masonry-layout/"}]

Based on the web search results, here's comprehensive information about CSS masonry layout in 2024-2025:

## Native Browser Support Status

No browser ships masonry layout unflagged in stable releases as of mid-2025. However, experimental support is available:

- Firefox has had the longest-running flag-gated support, dating back to Firefox 77.
- Safari Technology Preview has been iterating on its own build.
- Chrome's work has been split between evaluating both syntactic approaches.

## grid-template-rows: masonry Syntax

The grid-template-rows: masonry property, along with a competing masonry-template-tracks proposal, gives developers a pure-CSS path to dense, hole-free, variable-height layouts for the first time.

Mozilla and WebKit have favored the grid-template-rows: masonry extension of existing grid, which is what Firefox and Safari have shipped behind flags.

## Competing Proposals

The CSS Working Group is considering multiple approaches:
- The CSSWG has shown movement toward the display: masonry approach as of mid-2025
- To create the most common masonry layout where the columns are laid out in a grid, and the rows stack like masonry, use display: grid-lanes along with grid-template-columns.

## Production Alternatives

Until native support is stable, developers have several options:

1. **JavaScript Libraries**: Native CSS masonry isn't universally supported yet, making David DeSandro's Masonry.js or Isotope necessary for reliable implementations.

2. **Progressive Enhancement**: The @supports block ensures that only browsers understanding the masonry value apply it. All others render a standard grid with equal-height rows.

3. **Lightweight JavaScript Fallbacks**: Broad cross-browser support realistically takes 12-18 months to reach the threshold where you can safely drop the JS fallback. For new projects launching today, build with a lightweight JS library and keep the native CSS masonry path clean for a migration in late 2026 or early 2027.
